text: I’m leaving this comment here to come back to in a few years when I’m older and looking back at old memories. When I was about 8, I discovered Can Can, and as a young kid with ADD, I instantly fell in love with this meme song. I had also loved YouTube Poops, so it was natural that I would’ve found this video. I remember showing it to all my friends, my family, and constantly rewatching it. That must’ve been about 11 years ago, and here I am now. I’ve graduated high school, worked in a factory for a few months, then got into the trades while studying computer science in college. Life is hard, but I’ve made it this far already. I just wonder where I’ll be after the next decade.

text: "Fortran" from a bootleg game called Mario Lottery. He was used in another bootleg game (Flintstones NES hack) called "7 Grand Dad" which has Fortran with different colors in the title screen, and replaces Fred's head with Mario's head from Super Mario Bros. 3. Said bootleg has become popular (probably even popular than Mario Lottery, where "Fortran" made his debut) after Joel, a Vinesauce streamer, played it, and had a... rather infamous reaction to it, people loved it so much that it became a meme that was funny and good at first, but as time passed it became more boring and stale.
